revision.comment: Grundinfos
revision.text:
{{Infobox Ägyptische Gottheit |TITEL = Ta-djeser |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Neues Reich]] |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''{{Unicode|T3-ḏsr}}''
''Heiliges Land''
''Verbotenes Land der Lebenden''
''Totenreich'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' ist die [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] Bezeichnung des [[Totenreich]]s in der [[Duat]]. In der [[Ägyptische Mythologie|ägyptischen Mythologie]] galt Tja-deser als zugeteilte Region für die Verstorbenen, die nach dem positiven Urteil des [[Totengericht]]s mittels Überquerung des heiligen unterirdischen Sees der Reinigung erreicht wurde. Aus den Berichten des [[Nutbuch]]s geht hervor, dass die Götter sich als [[Chatiu-Dämonen|Chatiu]] im Totenreich regenerierten, um später gemeinsam mit [[Re (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]] neu geboren zu werden. == Literatur == * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. [[Kategorie:Ägyptische Mythologie]] [[Kategorie:Tod]]

{{Infobox Ägyptische Gottheit |TITEL = Ta-djeser |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Neues Reich]] |NAME = N16:D45

{{Dieser Artikel|befasst sich mit der altägyptischen Region in der Duat. Für die gleichnamige Bezeichnung der geografischen Region des Landes Israel, siehe [[Heiliges Land]].}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |TITEL = Ta-djeser |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Neues Reich]] |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''{{Unicode|T3-ḏsr}}''
''Heiliges Land'' /
''Lichtland'' /
''Verbotenes Land der Lebenden'' /
''Totenreich'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' ist die [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] Bezeichnung des [[Lichtland]]s in der [[Duat]]. In der [[Ägyptische Mythologie|ägyptischen Mythologie]] galt Ta-djeser als zugeteilte Region für die Verstorbenen, die nach dem positiven Urteil des [[Totengericht]]s mittels Überquerung des heiligen unterirdischen Sees der Reinigung erreicht wurde. Aus den Berichten des [[Nutbuch]]s geht hervor, dass die Götter sich als [[Chatiu-Dämonen|Chatiu]] im Totenreich regenerierten, um später gemeinsam mit [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]] neu geboren zu werden. == Siehe auch == * [[Sechet-iaru]] == Literatur == * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. [[Kategorie:Ort in der ägyptischen Mythologie]] [[Kategorie:Altägyptischer Totenkult]] [[Kategorie:Tod (Mythologie)]]

Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Ta-djeser]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day
[[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Ta-djeser]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |TITLE = Ta-djeser |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国}} |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''T3-ḏsr''
''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=ancient Egyptian|lt_de=altägyptische|lt_ja=古代エジプト的}} designation for the '''Land of Light''' in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト}}. In {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythological|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|lt_ja=エジプト神話的}}, Ta-djeser was regarded as the assigned region for the deceased, who, following the positive verdict of the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の審判}}, reached it by crossing the sacred subterranean lake of purification. Reports from the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書}} reveal that the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu Demons|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|ja|チャティウ・デーモン|lt=Chatiu|lt_de=Chatiu|lt_ja=チャティウ}} in the realm of the dead, to later be reborn together with {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|ラー (エジプト神話)|lt=Re|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=ラー}}. == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セヘト・イアル}} == Literature == * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study in Religious Astronomy in Egypt Using the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Cult of the Dead]] [[Category:Death (Mythology)]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:Ta-djeser]][[de:Ta-djeser]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Ta-djeser]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]]

In {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythological|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|lt_ja=エジプト神話的}}, Ta-djeser was regarded as the assigned region for the deceased, who, following the positive verdict of the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の審判}}, reached it by crossing the sacred subterranean lake of purification. Reports from the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書}} reveal that the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu Demons|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|ja|チャティウ・デーモン|lt=Chatiu|lt_de=Chatiu|lt_ja=チャティウ}} in the realm of the dead, to later be reborn together with {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|ラー (エジプト神話)|lt=Re|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=ラー}}. == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セヘト・イアル}} == Literature == * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. ''Holy Land'' /

{{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |TITLE = Ta-djeser |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''T3-ḏsr''
''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=ancient Egyptian|lt_de=altägyptische|lt_ja=古代エジプト的|12=draft|12=draft}} designation for the '''Land of Light''' in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythological|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|lt_ja=エジプト神話的|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ta-djeser was regarded as the assigned region for the deceased, who, following the positive verdict of the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|ja|死者の審判|12=draft|12=draft}}, reached it by crossing the sacred subterranean lake of purification. Reports from the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書|12=draft|12=draft}} reveal that the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu Demons|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|ja|チャティウ・デーモン|lt=Chatiu|lt_de=Chatiu|lt_ja=チャティウ|12=draft|12=draft}} in the realm of the dead, to later be reborn together with {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|ラー (エジプト神話)|lt=Re|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=ラー|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セヘト・イアル|12=draft|12=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study in Religious Astronomy in Egypt Using the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Cult of the Dead]] [[Category:Death (Mythology)]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:Ta-djeser]] [[de:Ta-djeser]] [[sn:Ta-djeser]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs Two-Column |TITLE = Ta-djeser |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=}} |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''T3-ḏsr''
''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=ancient Egyptian|lt_de=altägyptische|lt_ja=古代エジプト的|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}} designation for the '''Land of Light''' in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duat|qq=}}. In {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythological|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|lt_ja=エジプト神話的|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|qq=}}, Ta-djeser was regarded as the assigned region for the deceased, who, following the positive verdict of the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|1=Judgement (afterlife)|2=de|3=Totengericht|8=bn|9=বিচার (পরকাল)|10=ta|11=தீர்ப்பு (இறந்த பிறகு)|12=fa|13=قضاوت (پس از مرگ)|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Weighing of the Heart|ja|死者の審判|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Judgment of the Dead|qq=}}, reached it by crossing the sacred subterranean lake of purification. Reports from the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut|qq=}} reveal that the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu Demons|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|ja|チャティウ・デーモン|lt=Chatiu|lt_de=Chatiu|lt_ja=チャティウ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chatiu Demons|qq=}} in the realm of the dead, to later be reborn together with {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|ラー (エジプト神話)|lt=Re|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=ラー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Re (Egyptian Mythology)|qq=}}. ==Overview== {{draft categories| }} [[en::Ta‑djeser]] [[sn:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[sn:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[sn:Redirects to be cleared]] [[de:Ta-djeser|ja|タ・ジェセル|zh|塔杰塞尔]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs Two-Column |TITEL = Ta-djeser |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|zh|新王国}} |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''T3-ḏsr''
''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the ancient Egyptian term for the '''Land of Light''' in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|杜阿特}}. In {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|zh|埃及神话}}, Ta-djeser was the region assigned to the deceased, reached after a favorable verdict in the {{ill|Judgement of the Dead|de|Totengericht|1=Judgement (afterlife)|2=de|3=Totengericht|8=bn|9=বিচার (পরকাল)|10=ta|11=தீர்ப்பு (இறந்த பிறகு)|12=fa|13=قضاوت (پس از مرگ)|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Weighing of the Heart|ja|冥界の審判|zh|亡者审判}} by crossing the sacred underground Lake of Purification. According to the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書|zh|努特之书}}, the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|ja|チャティウ|zh|恰蒂乌}} demons in the realm of the dead, to be reborn later alongside {{ill|Ra|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|ラー|zh|拉}}. == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セケト-イアル|zh|塞切特-伊阿魯}} == Literature == * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 == External links == * [http://www.egyptology.com/reeder/enigma/tekenu8.html Further reading on the Duat and Ta-djeser] == References == [[Category:Places in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian funerary cult]] [[Category:Death (mythology)]] [[Category:translated pages]][[ja:タ＝ジェセル]][[de:Ta-djeser]] == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セヘト・イアル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-iaru|qq=}} == Literature == * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study in Religious Astronomy in Egypt Using the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. {{translated page|de|Ta-djeser|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Cult of the Dead]] [[Category:Death (Mythology)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Ta-djeser]] [[sn:Ta-djeser]][[sn:Ta-djeser]]}} [[sn:Ta-djeser]] [[sn:a-djeser]] [[de:Ta-djeser]] [[sn: failed]]

''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the ancient Egyptian term for the '''Land of Light''' in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|杜阿特}}. In {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|zh|埃及神话}}, Ta-djeser was the region assigned to the deceased, reached after a favorable verdict in the {{ill|Judgement of the Dead|de|Totengericht|1=Judgement (afterlife)|2=de|3=Totengericht|8=bn|9=বিচার (পরকাল)|10=ta|11=தீர்ப்பு (இறந்த பிறகு)|12=fa|13=قضاوت (پس از مرگ)|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Weighing of the Heart|ja|冥界の審判|zh|亡者审判}} by crossing the sacred underground Lake of Purification. According to the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書|zh|努特之书}}, the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|ja|チャティウ|zh|恰蒂乌}} demons in the realm of the dead, to be reborn later alongside {{ill|Ra|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|ラー|zh|拉}}. == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セケト-イアル|zh|塞切特-伊阿魯}} == Literature == * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 == External links == * [http://www.egyptology.com/reeder/enigma/tekenu8.html Further reading on the Duat and Ta-djeser] == References == [[Category:Places in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian funerary cult]] [[Category:Death (mythology)]] [[Category:translated pages]][[ja:タ＝ジェセル]][[de:Ta-djeser]] == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セヘト・イアル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-iaru|qq=}} == Literature == * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 == External links == * [http://www.egyptology.com/reeder/enigma/tekenu8.html Further reading on the Duat and Ta-djeser] == References == [[Category:Places in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian funerary cult]] [[Category:Death (mythology)]] [[Category:translated pages]][[ja:タ＝ジェセル]][[de:Ta-djeser]] == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|ja|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-iaru|qq=}} == Literature == * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs Two-Column |TITLE = Ta-djeser |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=}} |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''T3-ḏsr''
''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=ancient Egyptian|lt_de=altägyptische|lt_ja=古代エジプト的|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}} designation for the '''Land of Light''' in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duat|qq=}}. In {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythological|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|lt_ja=エジプト神話的|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|qq=}}, Ta-djeser was regarded as the assigned region for the deceased, who, following the positive verdict of the {{ill|Judgment of the Dead|de|Totengericht|1=Judgement (afterlife)|2=de|3=Totengericht|8=bn|9=বিচার (পরকাল)|10=ta|11=தீர்ப்பு (இறந்த பிறகு)|12=fa|13=قضاوت (پس از مرگ)|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Weighing of the Heart|ja|死者の審判|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Judgment of the Dead|qq=}}, reached it by crossing the sacred subterranean lake of purification. Reports from the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut|qq=}} reveal that the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu Demons|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ・デーモン|lt=Chatiu|lt_de=Chatiu|lt_ja=チャティウ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chatiu Demons|qq=}} in the realm of the dead, to later be reborn together with {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|ラー (エジプト神話)|lt=Re|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=ラー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Re (Egyptian Mythology)|qq=}}. ==Overview== {{draft categories| }} [[en::Ta-djeser]] [[sn:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[sn:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[sn:Redirects to be cleared]] [[de:Ta-djeser|ja|タ・ジェセル|zh|塔杰塞尔]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs Two-Column |TITEL = Ta-djeser |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|zh|新王国}} |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''T3-ḏsr''
''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the ancient Egyptian term for the '''Land of Light''' in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|zh|杜阿特}}. In {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|zh|埃及神话}}, Ta-djeser was the region assigned to the deceased, reached after a favorable verdict in the {{ill|Judgement of the Dead|de|Totengericht|1=Judgement (afterlife)|2=de|3=Totengericht|8=bn|9=বিচার (পরকাল)|10=ta|11=தீர்ப்பு (இறந்த பிறகு)|12=fa|13=قضاوت (پس از مرگ)|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Weighing of the Heart|ja|冥界の審判|zh|亡者审判}} by crossing the sacred underground Lake of Purification. According to the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書|zh|努特之书}}, the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ|zh|恰蒂乌}} demons in the realm of the dead, to be reborn later alongside {{ill|Ra|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|ラー|zh|拉}}. == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|ja|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|zh|塞切特-伊阿魯}} == Literature == * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 == External links == * [http://www.egyptology.com/reeder/enigma/tekenu8.html Further reading on the Duat and Ta-djeser] == References == [[Category:Places in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian funerary cult]] [[Category:Death (mythology)]] [[Category:translated pages]][[ja:タ＝ジェセル]][[de:Ta-djeser]] == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|ja|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-iaru|qq=}} == Literature == * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study in Religious Astronomy in Egypt Using the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. {{translated page|de|Ta-djeser|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Cult of the Dead]] [[Category:Death (Mythology)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Ta-djeser]] [[sn:Ta-djeser]][[sn:Ta-djeser]]}} [[sn:Ta-djeser]] [[sn:a-djeser]] [[de:Ta-djeser]] [[sn: failed]]

According to the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|ja|ヌトの書|zh|努特之书}}, the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ|zh|恰蒂乌|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu}} demons in the realm of the dead, to be reborn later alongside {{ill|Ra|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|ラー|zh|拉}}. == See also == * {{ill|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|ja|Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru|2=de|3=Sechet-iaru|4=ja|5=セヘト・イアル|5=アアル|6=fr|7=Champs d'Ialou|8=ru|9=Поля Иалу|10=zh|11=雅盧|lt=Sechet-iaru|zh|塞切特-伊阿魯}} == Literature == * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITLE = Ta-djeser |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]] |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''T3-ḏsr''
''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] designation for the '''Land of Light''' in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. In [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythological]], Ta-djeser was regarded as the assigned region for the deceased, who, following the positive verdict of the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]], reached it by crossing the sacred subterranean lake of purification. Reports from the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] reveal that the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu Demons|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ・デーモン|lt=Chatiu|lt_de=Chatiu|lt_ja=チャティウ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chatiu Demons|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シンプル}} in the realm of the dead, to later be reborn together with [[:en:Ra|Re]]. ==Overview== {{draft categories| }} [[sn:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[sn:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[sn:Redirects to be cleared]] [[de:Ta-djeser|ja|タ・ジェセル|zh|塔杰塞尔]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Ta-djeser |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]] |NAME = N16:D45
Ta-djeser
''T3-ḏsr''
''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the ancient Egyptian term for the '''Land of Light''' in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. In [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]], Ta-djeser was the region assigned to the deceased, reached after a favorable verdict in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] by crossing the sacred underground Lake of Purification. According to the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ|zh|恰蒂乌|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demons in the realm of the dead, to be reborn later alongside [[:en:Ra|Ra]]. == See also == * [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] == Literature == * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 == External links == * [http://www.egyptology.com/reeder/enigma/tekenu8.html Further reading on the Duat and Ta-djeser] == References == [[Category:translated pages]][[ja:タ＝ジェセル]][[de:Ta-djeser]] == See also == * [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] == Literature == * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study in Religious Astronomy in Egypt Using the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. {{translated page|de|Ta-djeser|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Ta-djeser]] [[sn:Ta-djeser]][[sn:Ta-djeser]]}} [[sn:Ta-djeser]] [[sn:a-djeser]] [[de:Ta-djeser]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian funerary practices]] [[Category:Tod (Mythologie)]]

''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] designation for the '''Land of Light''' in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. ''T3-ḏsr''
''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] designation for the '''Land of Light''' in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. In [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythological]], Ta-djeser was regarded as the assigned region for the deceased, who, following the positive verdict of the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]], reached it by crossing the sacred subterranean lake of purification. Reports from the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] reveal that the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu Demons|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ・デーモン|lt=Chatiu|lt_de=Chatiu|lt_ja=チャティウ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chatiu Demons|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シンプル}} in the realm of the dead, to later be reborn together with [[:en:Ra|Re]]. ==Overview== {{draft categories| }} [[de:Ta-djeser|ja|タ・ジェセル|zh|塔杰塞尔]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Ta-djeser |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]] |NAME = N16:D45

''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the ancient Egyptian term for the '''Land of Light''' in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. In [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]], Ta-djeser was the region assigned to the deceased, reached after a favorable verdict in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] by crossing the sacred underground Lake of Purification. According to the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ|zh|恰蒂乌|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demons in the realm of the dead, to be reborn later alongside [[:en:Ra|Ra]]. == See also == * [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] == Literature == * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. According to the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ|zh|恰蒂乌|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demons in the realm of the dead, to be reborn later alongside [[:en:Ra|Ra]]. == See also == * [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] == Literature == * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] designation for the '''Land of Light''' in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. In [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythological]], Ta-djeser was regarded as the assigned region for the deceased, who, following the positive verdict of the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]], reached it by crossing the sacred subterranean lake of purification. Reports from the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] reveal that the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu Demons|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ・デーモン|lt=Chatiu|lt_de=Chatiu|lt_ja=チャティウ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chatiu Demons|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シンプル}} in the realm of the dead, to later be reborn together with [[:en:Ra|Re]]. ==Overview== {{draft categories| }} [[de:Ta-djeser|ja|タ・ジェセル|zh|塔杰塞尔]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Ta-djeser |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]] |NAME = N16:D45

''Holy Land'' /
''Land of Light'' /
''Forbidden Land of the Living'' /
''Realm of the Dead'' }} '''Ta-djeser''' is the ancient Egyptian term for the '''Land of Light''' in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. In [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]], Ta-djeser was the region assigned to the deceased, reached after a favorable verdict in the [[:en:Judgement (afterlife)|Weighing of the Heart]] by crossing the sacred underground Lake of Purification. According to the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], the gods regenerated as {{ill|Chatiu|de|Chatiu-Dämonen|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja|チャティウ|zh|恰蒂乌|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|1=Chatiu demons|2=de|3=Chatiu-Dämonen|4=es|5=Demonios chatiu|6=pt|7=Daemons-Chatiu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demons in the realm of the dead, to be reborn later alongside [[:en:Ra|Ra]]. == See also == * [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] == Literature == * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''.
